layman_headline: Market fear and a fresh geopolitical shock make it too risky to jump in right now.
layman_explanation: Bitcoin just dropped below $63,000 after news of a U.S. strike on Iran, dragging the entire crypto market down. The Fear & Greed Index sits at 27 (Fear), and Bitcoin dominance is high at 56%, meaning altcoins are getting hit even harder. While some coins like ETH and LDO look oversold on paper, the broad market structure is bearish and there's no clear catalyst to reverse it yet. Patience here protects capital.
layman_action: Stay on the sidelines and keep cash ready. If ETH drops to the $1,700-$1,750 range with stabilizing volume, that could be a better entry. Don't chase falling knives in a fearful market.
expert_reasoning: The macro backdrop is decisively risk-off: BTC is down 3% on Iran strike news, altcoins are bleeding harder (HYPE -11%, BONK -9.5%, TIA -9.7%), and Fear & Greed is at 27. ETH's RSI at 35 is approaching oversold territory — a reliable signal historically (80% win rate from our data) — but the chart structure is still bearish with price below all major MAs. The Bullish Marubozu on the chart was from the prior session and today's -5.1% drop likely negates it. LDO's +18% weekly run despite bearish MA structure suggests it's a dead cat bounce rather than a sustained reversal. HYPE has the best chart bias (+1.1) but RSI at 59 and -11% daily drop signal distribution, not accumulation. I'm waiting for ETH to find support near $1,700-$1,750 where a proper RSI oversold bounce with volume confirmation would offer a high-conviction entry.
risk_factors: Geopolitical escalation (Iran situation) could worsen, sending BTC below $60K and dragging all alts further
BTC dominance at 56.1% and rising means alt-season is nowhere close — altcoin entries face structural headwinds
Fear & Greed at 27 is improving from 25 but could easily reverse back into Extreme Fear on another shock
watch_list: ETH price action around $1,700-$1,750 support zone — a hold there with RSI below 30 would be a strong buy signal
BTC reaction to Iran/geopolitical developments over the next 24-48 hours
Whether Fear & Greed continues its improving trend or reverses back toward Extreme Fear
market_context: Crypto is in a broad risk-off mode driven by a U.S. strike on Iran and Trump's China uncertainty. BTC dominance at 56.1% is crushing altcoins disproportionately, and Fear & Greed at 27 suggests we haven't yet reached capitulation levels where contrarian entries shine.
context_banner: Geopolitical shock drives crypto-wide sell-off — patience is the highest-conviction trade until fear peaks and support holds.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 35 is approaching oversold but not yet at the sub-30 level where our 80% win rate historical data kicks in — waiting for deeper oversold confirmation.
Fear & Greed at 27 (Fear) with improving trend from 25, but geopolitical risk could easily reverse this — not yet at extreme fear capitulation levels.
Bitcoin under $63K after U.S. strike on Iran and Trump China comments — dual macro headwinds creating broad risk-off environment across all crypto assets.
Citadel Securities $400M investment in Crypto.com at $20B valuation signals long-term institutional conviction, but won't stop short-term sell pressure.
No volume breakout signals detected on any candidate — sell-offs are broad and indiscriminate rather than showing accumulation patterns.
